heroHeadline: IMF Warns of Looming Global Inflation Crisis; US-China Tariffs at 104%/84%; JPMorgan Recession Probability at 65%
heroSubtitle: April 9: The IMF issued a warning of a looming global inflation crisis driven by the US-Israel war on Iran, adding a major multilateral institution's assessment to Wall Street stagflation signals. This follows the US-China dual tariff shock: Trump's effective rate on Chinese goods at ~104%, China retaliation at 84% — the deepest bilateral trade barrier since the 1930s Smoot-Hawley era. JPMorgan recession probability stands at 65% (highest since pandemic); Moody's Analytics crossed 50%. The FOMC faces a stagflation trap ahead of its April 29–30 meeting: core PCE at 3.06% prohibits rate cuts, while slowing growth demands stimulus.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Conference Board, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Yale Budget Lab, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, Cleveland Fed Inflation Nowcasting
